publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-12-04 23:26:58 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, photographs, newspaper clippings, biographical material and ephemera, bound volumes and original writings by Paul Brunton, including journals, notebooks and manuscripts.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Paul Brunton was a British author who devoted his life to communicating the spiritual teachings and practices of Eastern traditions in a form suited to the contemporary Western reader. He wrote eleven books, and travelled to lecture and to interview hundreds of teachers, fakirs, saints, and sages around the globe. After retiring, he continued his philosophical writing for several decades. Most of it is contained in this archive.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to Folder 2 in Box 88 restricted until 2046.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Brunton papers, #8692. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Website to the digital complement of the Paul Brunton papers https://www.pbarchives.org/

controlaccess.geogname: Tibet Autonomous Region (China) -- Description and travel.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters include from Maharaja of Mysore, Maharaja of Kashmir. Pictures included as well.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of yogis, various holymen, and scenes of India.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Paul Brunton in Mysore, Japan, and Europe.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Paul Brunton and various holymen.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Tibetan monasteries, Himalayas, and Mysore royal family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of sacred and courtly architecture from Delhi, Travancore, and Mysore.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of architecture and daily life from Mysore, Colombo, and Sri Lanka.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Paul Brunton, various holymen, and royalty from India.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of various mystics and spirtualists.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of architecture and religious life from Angkor Wat, Bangkok, Cambodia, Indonesia, Burma, Indochina, and Japan.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains photographs of Sri Ramana Maharshi and varoius holymen across India and Tiruvannamalai.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Paul Brunton wrote the introduction to this book.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 127th copy. 200 copies were published by Short Path Press in Earlville, New York and printed by Worden Press in Brookfield, New York.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Title page reads "Unpublished Commentaries by Sri Ramana Maharshi" - however, this has been published by the Ramanashram since.
